violations.text: A wiping cloth was observed in the hand-washing sink in the service area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of pink biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was cleaned and sanitized taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; ice disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Working containers of Macha and sauces are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

The door to the outside, located in the building trash room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/26/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice bin) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC placed bar mat under ice scoop. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026
The hand-washing sink near espresso machine was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

Chicken Salad and Roasted Tomatoes were observed held at 43 and 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per employee, foods in unit were recently placed or was recently processed. Ambient of unit was 49 degrees Fahrenheit. All TCS foods were moved to other refrigeration during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

Prep Unit Lowboy was observed at an ambient of 49 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was taken out of service during time of inspection. TCS/PHF foods cannot be placed in unit until equipment is properly adjusted, repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of food residue or soil residue: 1. Interior of prep unit 2. Basin of automatic glass rinser New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue and or debris: 1. The floors in the walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer 2. The vent in the walk-in freezer (ice accumulation) The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
